summary: This commit adds two new, internal, protected methods to `DocCommentParser` to support Javadoc snippet error reporting. As such, it is not generalizable into a TriggerPattern DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Bug 579375 - CompilationUnitResolver.getProjectPath(IJavaProject) is not correct

Change-Id: Ib540ad7114a00d8e3c93aa4e21e269746a30d6a7
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/+/192199
Tested-by: Sravan Kumar Lakkimsetti <sravankumarl@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-by: Sravan Kumar Lakkimsetti <sravankumarl@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-by: Jay Arthanareeswaran <jarthana@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: The rule addresses a pattern of modernizing/simplifying how a project path is obtained using Eclipse JDT Core APIs, replacing a verbose concatenation with a direct method call. This falls under API migration within the Eclipse context.
summary: This commit simplifies the logic for obtaining a project path in JDT Core, replacing a lengthy concatenation of `IWorkspaceRoot.getLocation().toString()` and `IProject.getPath().toString()` with a direct call to `IProject.getLocation().toString()`. This is a straightforward API modernization that improves readability and reduces verbosity, directly implementable as a DSL rule. No source version guard is needed as `IProject.getLocation()` is an older, well-established API.

commitMessage:
Bug 579375 - NPE in CompilationUnitResolver.getProjectPath(IJavaProject)

- don't resolve getLocation()
- don't use IPath.toString(), use toOSString() instead

Change-Id: I39f5e415df42d5c77cbe7ac32c48886d749ef776
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/+/192266
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Andrey Loskutov <loskutov@gmx.de>
categoryReason: The rule replaces a specific Eclipse API method call with another, improving correctness and addressing a potential bug in Eclipse Platform code.
summary: This commit replaces `IPath.toString()` with `IPath.toOSString()` when retrieving a project's location. `toOSString()` provides the path in the native operating system format, which is typically more appropriate for file system operations and prevents potential issues with internal path representations, as implied by the NPE fix in the commit message. The rule will guard on `instanceof($path, "org.eclipse.core.runtime.IPath")` to ensure it only applies to Eclipse `IPath` objects. This is a direct, semantically safer replacement.

* #57 - Initial Java 19 addition - experimental

* #57 Correcting the API Baseline errors - JEP 427 switch grammar

* #57 Correcting the API Baseline errors - JEP 427 switch grammar

* #57 Correcting the failures - JEP 427 switch grammar
categoryReason: This rule addresses the deprecation and modernization of Eclipse JDT Core API constants related to Java Language Specification levels.
summary: This commit deprecates the `AST.JLS18` constant in the Eclipse JDT Core DOM API and updates the internal 'latest' JLS level to JLS19. The proposed rule automates the replacement of the deprecated `org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.AST.JLS18` constant with the recommended `org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.AST.getJLSLatest()` method, ensuring clients use the most up-to-date API for obtaining the latest supported Java Language Specification level. This aligns with standard JDT API modernization practices.

commitMessage:
[19] JEP 427 Switch Pattern Third Preview Grammar Changes (#74)

Fix the API errors by adding filters
irrelevantReason: This commit primarily involves internal JDT Core DOM API evolution to support new Java 19 language features (JEP 427 Switch Pattern). Changes include adding internal JLS19 constants, updating internal API level maps, adding the 'when' keyword for AST parsing, and modifying AST flattener logic to correctly print the new `GuardedPattern` syntax. These are not user-facing code transformations that can be generalized into a TriggerPattern DSL rule. The flattener change from `&&` to `when` is for internal AST printing to reflect new language syntax, not a transformation of `A && B` to `A when B` in existing user code, as `A when B` is a new language construct.
summary: This commit is not relevant for DSL rule mining as it focuses on internal compiler support for a new Java language feature (JLS19 Switch Patterns) rather than refactoring existing Java code patterns.

commitMessage: [19] Grammar and compiler AST changes for record patterns #104

commitMessage:
Improve type compatibility checks in pattern resolution #134 (#144)

Pattern resolution and code generation must take care of scenarios where
the pattern signature could be using a subtype or a super type of the
record declaraion.
irrelevantReason: This change is an internal JDT Core DOM implementation detail related to AST conversion for pattern resolution. It is not a user-facing code transformation pattern that can be generalized into a DSL rule.
summary: The commit modifies an internal implementation detail within JDT's ASTConverter, specifically adjusting the source range calculation from `pattern.typePattern.sourceStart` to `pattern.local.sourceStart` for record patterns. This change improves the internal representation of AST nodes for pattern resolution, likely related to supporting newer Java language features. This is an internal compiler fix/improvement and does not represent a reusable code transformation pattern for end-users, thus not applicable for TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Pattern Name is Optional in RecordPattern (#255)

see https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/issues/94
irrelevantReason: This commit modifies the internal Eclipse JDT DOM API and AST construction logic for Record Patterns (Java 19+), not application-level Java code. It's about how the JDT represents new language features, not transforming existing code.
summary: This commit modifies the internal JDT DOM API (`RecordPattern.java` and `ASTConverter.java`) to indicate that the pattern name in a Record Pattern is optional, aligning with the Java 19+ language specification. These changes are to the core AST structure and its internal conversion logic within the Eclipse JDT itself. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed to match and transform patterns within *existing Java source code*, not to modify the JDT's internal representation of language features. Therefore, this commit does not represent a transformation pattern suitable for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Use ToolFactory.createScanner() with source compliance set if possible

To avoid InvalidInputException if the source code contains elements not
covered by Java 1.3 JLS, specify proper source/target arguments while
creating scanner.

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/issues/292
categoryReason: The change involves modernization and correction of internal JDT API usage within the JDT core.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support: 1. Conditional replacements that introduce `if/else` control flow. 2. Declaration and assignment of new local variables within an expression replacement. 3. Chaining method calls on matched objects (`source.getJavaProject()`) to retrieve dynamic arguments for a constructor/method call (`project.getOption(...)`). These are all complex statement-level transformations that go beyond current expression-level rewrite capabilities.
summary: This commit replaces a simple `ToolFactory.createScanner` call with a conditional block that dynamically determines `sourceLevel` and `complianceLevel` from the `IJavaProject` to use a more appropriate `createScanner` overload. This transformation introduces new variable declarations and an `if-else` statement, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for expression-level rewrites. It's a significant internal API usage improvement but requires advanced structural rewrite capabilities not present in the DSL.

commitMessage: Support for @highlight and @replace in external snippets. Fixes #150 (#298)
irrelevantReason: This commit modifies the internal implementation of the JDT `DocCommentParser`. The changes involve a method signature refactoring, local variable renaming, and adding new internal control flow logic specific to parsing Javadoc snippets. These are not generalizable code transformation patterns applicable to user code via the TriggerPattern DSL. It's an internal refactoring within JDT itself.
summary: This commit introduces internal changes to JDT's `DocCommentParser` to support new features in Javadoc snippets. It involves a method signature change, local variable renaming, and adding complex conditional logic for internal parsing state, none of which are generalizable patterns suitable for TriggerPattern DSL rules. It's an internal implementation detail, not a code transformation for user-facing code.
